entries.content: Compares two measures of corporate profitability: (1) Corrected profit rate (rcorp) using imputed-interest-adjusted NOS and GPIM total capital stock (including IRS inventories); (2) Conventional NIPA profit rate (rcorpnipa) using standard NIPA corporate profits and BEA current-cost net stock. Also computes corresponding profit shares (NOS/VA for corrected, P_nipa/VA_nipa for conventional).

BEA NIPA Tables 1.14 (corporate accounts), 7.11 (imputed interest). BEA Fixed Asset Tables 6.1-6.8 (capital stocks). IRS Statistics of Income (corporate inventories). All combined in Shaikh's GPIM methodology for total capital stock KTC.

Fig 6.2 shows corrected vs conventional corporate profit rates 1947-2011. The corrected rate is systematically lower due to both the imputed interest adjustment (raising measured profit via interest reclassification) and the GPIM capital stock (larger denominator including inventories and historical-cost adjustments). Fig 6.6 normalizes for capacity utilization.

citations.full: Bureau of Economic Analysis. NIPA Table 1.14: Gross Value Added of Domestic Corporate Business.
Internal Revenue Service. Statistics of Income: Corporation Income Tax Returns. Various years.

methodology_summary: S027 extracts four columns from S013 (Table II-7): S013J (corrected rcorp = NOS_corp/KTC_corp(-1)), S013O (conventional rcorpnipa = P_corpnipa/KNC_corpbea(-1)), S013I (corrected profit share), S013N (conventional profit share). The divergence between corrected and conventional measures demonstrates the quantitative importance of Shaikh's three adjustments: imputed interest, inventory inclusion, and GPIM capital stock revaluation.
